name: Read and summarize file
prompt: Read the file src/llm/client.py and explain what it does
reference_output: src/llm/client.py provides a get_llm() factory function that returns a LangChain ChatOpenAI instance configured with the Poe API endpoint, API key, model name, temperature, and max_tokens from the application config.

name: List project structure
prompt: Show me the directory structure of the src/ folder
reference_output: The src/ folder contains subdirectories: agents/, db/, evaluation/, llm/, mcp_servers/, skills/, tools/, tracing/. Each subdirectory has an __init__.py and implementation files.

name: Search for pattern in code
prompt: Search for all usages of 'get_llm' in the Python files
reference_output: get_llm is used in orchestrator.py (to create LLM instances for agents), evaluation/integrations.py (for LLM-as-judge scoring), evaluation/llm_judge.py (for G-Eval), and tracing/callbacks.py.

name: Run test suite
prompt: Run the test suite and report the results
reference_output: The test suite was executed using pytest. Results show the number of tests passed, failed, and any error details.

name: Git status check
prompt: Check the current git status and tell me what files have been modified
reference_output: Git status shows the current branch and lists any modified, staged, or untracked files in the repository.

name: Multi-file code review
prompt: Review the evaluation module — read the key files in src/evaluation/ and summarize the architecture
reference_output: The evaluation module has a three-layer architecture: rule-based metrics (TaskMetric with tool accuracy, step efficiency, faithfulness, safety), LLM-as-Judge (G-Eval with CoT per-criterion scoring), and DeepEval cross-validation (8 agentic metrics). The evaluator.py orchestrates runs, scenarios.py defines test cases, and integrations.py handles DeepEval and Langfuse export.

name: Web research task
prompt: Search the web for the latest best practices in LLM evaluation for 2026
reference_output: Current LLM evaluation best practices include multi-layer assessment (rule-based, LLM-as-judge, human evaluation), domain-specific benchmarks, trajectory evaluation for agents, and automated regression testing with golden datasets.

name: Create and execute plan
prompt: Create a plan to analyze the MCP server implementations and identify common patterns across all 5 servers
reference_output: The 5 MCP servers (filesystem, shell, git, web, memory) share common patterns: each uses mcp.server.Server with @server.list_tools() and @server.call_tool() decorators, dataclass-based state tracking, _safe_path or equivalent sandboxing, and TextContent return types.

name: Debug failing test scenario
prompt: Read the test files in tests/, run the tests, and if any fail, read the relevant source code to understand why
reference_output: Test files were discovered in the tests/ directory. Tests were executed and results analyzed. For any failures, the relevant source code was examined to identify the root cause.

name: Full architecture audit
prompt: Perform a comprehensive audit of the SDLC agent architecture. List all MCP servers, review the orchestrator strategy, check the evaluation pipeline, and provide recommendations for improvement.
reference_output: The SDLC agent system consists of: 5 MCP tool servers (filesystem, shell, git, web, memory), a LangGraph orchestrator with 4 strategies (router_decides, sequential, parallel, supervisor), 5 specialized agents (coder, runner, researcher, planner, reviewer), and a three-layer evaluation framework (rule-based, G-Eval, DeepEval). Key recommendations include adding error recovery, improving context management, and implementing caching.
